wall_type: TIME (150s probe timeout on the chunked-prefill KV build), NOT VRAM
wall_note: APA-decode probes chunked-prefill the full L-1 KV then do one decode step. At L>=17408 the chunked build exceeds the 150s probe cap and the driver classifies the timeout as OOM. VRAM was NOT the wall: last-solid 16384 used only 3403 MiB (poller peak 8955 MiB across the whole ladder, ~3 GiB headroom on the 12 GiB card). This is a measurement-protocol wall (probe-timeout on a serial chunked build), reported honestly as time-bounded, not a memory ceiling. A longer probe timeout would push it higher; not spent tonight (power-safety <=10min/run + budget).
